prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. This account focuses on the developments in the northern region of Afghanistan that occurred from April 2009 through 2014. Despite this particular area of the nation having generally experienced a greater degree of tranquility compared to the active conflict zones in the southern and eastern parts, hostilities reignited in 2008. During this period, German military personnel stationed in the region faced an increased frequency of assaults, which tragically resulted in the fatalities of multiple soldiers. The worsening security conditions, which had previously been constrained by national restrictions, compelled the German-commanded Regional Command North to initiate a sequence of military actions aimed at confronting the escalating insurgency. Coordinated military efforts were set into motion following an insurgent assault on PRT Kunduz, which took place mere moments after German Chancellor Angela Merkel had concluded her visit and departed. Over the subsequent two years, the German military presence was expanded twofold, and supplementary troops from the American International Security Assistance Force (ISAF) contingent were deployed.
B. During the period of April 2009 to 2014, Northern Afghanistan experienced heightened tensions due to frequent attacks on German soldiers. This resulted in the deaths of several soldiers. The insurgent attack on PRT Kunduz within minutes of Chancellor Angela Merkel's departure prompted the German-led Regional Command North to launch a series of operations to address the escalating insurgency. Within two years, the German presence would be reinforced and reinforcements from the American ISAF contingent were provided.
C. The following addresses the events in Northern Afghanistan between April 2009 and 2014. While this part of the country had long been relatively peaceful compared to the all-out war zones of the south and east, tensions would flare up again in 2008 when the German soldiers deployed to the area came under attack more often, leading to the deaths of the several soldiers. Previously hindered by national caveats, the deteroriating security situation prompted the German-led Regional Command North to launch a series of operations to take on the rising insurgency. Concerted operations began after an insurgent attack on PRT Kunduz within minutes of German Chancellor Angela Merkel's departure from a visit. Within two years, the German presence would be doubled and additional reinforcements from the American ISAF contingent

Answer:

The worsening security conditions, which had previously been constrained by national restrictions, compelled the German-commanded Regional Command North to initiate a sequence of military actions aimed at confronting the escalating insurgency. Coordinated military efforts were set into motion following an insurgent assault on PRT Kunduz, which took place mere moments after German Chancellor Angela Merkel had concluded her visit and departed. Over the subsequent two years, the German military presence was expanded twofold, and supplementary troops from the American International Security Assistance Force (ISAF) contingent were deployed.

The insurgent attack on PRT Kunduz within minutes of Chancellor Angela Merkel's departure prompted the German-led Regional Command North to launch a series of operations to address the escalating insurgency. Within two years, the German presence would be reinforced and reinforcements from the American ISAF contingent were provided.
